rationale: The cited paper is treated as one of the closest antecedents in the multimedia cross-channel advertising literature, not just a background mention. The citing paper summarizes Dinner et al.'s specific findings (e.g., paid search effects on online and in-store sales), uses it to motivate its conceptual setup around search and purchase timing, and repeatedly contrasts its own design against Dinner et al.'s aggregate single-brand approach. It also benchmarks its empirical outcomes (elasticities and ROI patterns) against Dinner et al., showing direct intellectual continuity.
relation_description: The citing paper explicitly builds on Dinner et al. (2014) by extending that line of work from aggregate time-series evidence to customer-level panel modeling with multiple retailer-brands, separate media effects, and richer heterogeneity. It uses the cited study as a key prior reference for framing, model motivation, and result comparison.
